summary: Open Library provides public HTTP APIs for catalog search, work/edition/author metadata retrieval, legacy bibkey lookups, and cover image fetching from Open Library records.

auth.instructions: No API key or OAuth token is required for the documented read APIs. Open Library asks clients to send a descriptive User-Agent with contact information and follow published usage guidance, including rate limits (100 requests per 5 minutes by default, up to 300 per 5 minutes for identified clients).

endpoints.name: search-books
endpoints.description: Searches books and editions in Open Library's index.

endpoints.name: get-work
endpoints.description: Returns metadata for a work by Open Library work ID (OL...W).

endpoints.name: get-edition
endpoints.description: Returns metadata for an edition by Open Library edition ID (OL...M).

endpoints.name: get-author
endpoints.description: Returns author metadata by Open Library author ID (OL...A).

endpoints.name: lookup-books-by-bibkey
endpoints.description: Legacy API for looking up one or more books by bibkeys such as ISBN, OCLC, LCCN, OLID.

endpoints.name: get-cover-image
endpoints.description: Fetches a cover image by cover ID and size from the Covers API host (use this path against https://covers.openlibrary.org).
